name: Via de la Plata - ride around the walled city of Galisteo
name_original: Vía de la Plata – rodea a la ciudad amurallada de Galisteo
region_intro: Hiking around Plasencia offers diverse terrain in the northern part of Spain's Cáceres province. The region is characterized by the fertile Jerte Valley, known for its river and cherry orchards, and Monfragüe National Park, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve featuring Mediterranean forests, cork and holm oaks, and dramatic quartzite ridges. The landscape is shaped by the Jerte, Tajo, and Tiétar rivers, providing varied environments for exploration. Best hiking trails around Plasencia The most popular hiking route is The Pilones of the Garganta de los Infiernos (Gorge of the Hells), a 4.0 miles (6.4 km) trail that takes about 2 hours to complete. This moderate route leads to natural rock pools carved by river erosion. Another top favourite among local hikers is Garganta de las Nogaleas Trail, an easy 3.1 miles (5.0 km) path. This trail features waterfalls and offers panoramic views within the Jerte Valley. Local hikers also love the Monfragüe Castle Trail, a 4.3 miles (6.9 km) trail leading through Mediterranean forests and offering views from the historic Monfragüe Castle, often completed in about 2 hours 25 minutes. Hiking around Plasencia is defined by the river valleys of the Jerte, Tajo, and Tiétar, the Mediterranean forests of Monfragüe National Park, and the natural pools of Garganta de los Infiernos. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Plasencia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 600 reviews. More than 4000 hikers have used komoot to explore Plasencia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of hiking trails can I expect around Plasencia?
A: The region around Plasencia offers a wide variety of hiking trails, ranging from easy strolls to more challenging ascents. You'll find diverse terrain, including the fertile Jerte Valley with its river and cherry orchards, and the dramatic landscapes of Monfragüe National Park, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve characterized by Mediterranean forests and quartzite ridges. In total, there are over 125 hiking routes available, catering to all experience levels.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Plasencia has many easy trails. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990319686/garganta-de-las-nogaleas-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garganta de las Nogaleas Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.1-mile (5.0 km) path featuring stunning waterfalls and panoramic views within the Jerte Valley. There are 41 easy routes in total, perfect for a relaxed outing.

Q: Where can I find trails with waterfalls or natural pools?
A: The Jerte Valley is renowned for its water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2359868754/the-pilones-of-the-garganta-de-los-infiernos-gorge-of-the-hells" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pilones of the Garganta de los Infiernos&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate route leading to natural rock pools carved by river erosion, which are great for swimming in summer. You can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4204503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogaleas Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; or the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4360769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caozo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a challenge, the region offers 14 difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990360926/monfraguee-castle-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monfragüe Castle Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, is a 4.3-mile (6.9 km) difficult trail leading through Mediterranean forests and offering rewarding views from the historic Monfragüe Castle. Another option is the historic and demanding 25-kilometer Carlos V Trail mentioned in the region research.

Q: What are the best times of year to go hiking in Plasencia?
A: Plasencia offers unique hiking experiences throughout the year. Spring is particularly spectacular in the Jerte Valley when its two million cherry trees blossom, creating a white blanket across the landscape. Autumn also provides memorable hikes with its golden leaves. The distinct seasons offer varied scenery and conditions for exploration.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Plasencia?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. A notable one is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990441671/cerro-gimio-monfraguee-national-park-circular-via-the-green-route-from-villarreal-de-san-carlos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Gimio - Monfragüe National Park circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which offers views over the mouth of the Tiétar into the Tagus. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990727089/cascada-del-caozo-circular-from-valdastillas-pr-cc-32" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada del Caozo circular from Valdastillas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of landmarks or viewpoints can I see on the trails?
A: Hiking around Plasencia offers numerous opportunities to discover landmarks and stunning viewpoints. In Monfragüe National Park, you can visit the historic Monfragüe Castle or the Salto del Gitano Viewpoint, a 300-meter high cliff overlooking the Tagus River. The Jerte Valley also provides incredible natural viewpoints, such as those found on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990739666/mojon-blanco-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mojón Blanco Trail&lt;/a&gt;, offering expansive valley views.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Plasencia?
A: The hiking routes in Plasencia are highly rated by the komoot community, with an impressive average score of 4.5 stars from over 600 reviews. More than 4000 hikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the natural beauty of the Jerte Valley and the rich biodiversity of Monfragüe National Park.

Q: Are there any trails that are particularly good for birdwatching?
A: Monfragüe National Park is recognized as one of Europe's best birdwatching areas. Trails within the park, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1195580582/monfraguee-castle-trail-red-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monfragüe Castle Trail (Red Route)&lt;/a&gt;, offer excellent opportunities to spot diverse bird species, including Spanish Imperial eagles, Egyptian vultures, and Griffon vultures, especially from viewpoints like Salto del Gitano.

Q: Can I find trails that include historical sites?
A: Yes, the region has a rich history reflected in its trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990360926/monfraguee-castle-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monfragüe Castle Trail&lt;/a&gt; leads to a historic castle with panoramic views. Additionally, the wider region features the Vía de la Plata (Silver Route), a long-distance path following a historic Roman road with remnants like the Arco de Cáparra, offering a blend of nature and history.

Q: Are there any trails that are particularly long or offer a full-day adventure?
A: For a longer adventure, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990746024/castillo-de-mirabel-circular-through-the-umbria-del-berbechoso-monfraguee-national-park-biosphere-pr-cc-66" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Mirabel circular route&lt;/a&gt; in Monfragüe National Park, which spans over 15 kilometers. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1497795617/ruta-de-los-pilones-natural-reserve-of-the-garganta-de-los-infiernos-hell-s-gorge" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de los Pilones&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 13.3 km trail through the Garganta de los Infiernos Natural Reserve.
tips: Crowning the Jerte as it passes through the town of Galisteo, in Cáceres, you can see a medieval bridge. It was built in 1546 by order of Garcí Fernández Manrique de Lara, third Count of Osorno. That is why there are those who also call it the Manrique Lara bridge. Built with granite ashlars, it has seven arches and a temple with the image of Saint Paul.

Church of Ntra. Sra. de la Asunción, built in the 13th century, following plans of the Mudejar Romanesque of Castilla y León. Its apse is formed by two superimposed bodies of blind brick arches. The nave of this church was reformed in the 16th century.

waypoints.name: Church of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción
